TextENGNew: "Oishi-san, are you there? There's an outside call for you — from a civilian."
"Wh-Why can't I declare ron?! Ah, please wait, Oishi-saaaan!"
"Oh, excuse me for a moment! I'll be right back!"
changeReason: Old translation adds 'thank you' not in original; new translation correctly renders ちょいと失礼 as 'excuse me for a moment,' better matching the Japanese.

TextENGNew: The man sitting across from Oishi was waving a receiver at him.
"Outside line. Public telephone."
"Aah, terribly sorry! ...Sorry for the wait, this is Oishi. Hello?"
changeReason: Old translation renders もしもし as 'Who is this?' while new correctly translates it as 'Hello?' — a standard Japanese phone greeting, not a demand for identification.

TextENGNew: "I-Is that you, Oishi-san?! Hello?!"
"Well, if it isn't Maebara-san. Good afternoon, what brings you to call?"
"...I-I thought this was going to be an ordinary bonus scenario at first. ...But from the very start, everything has been completely wrong!!"
changeReason: Old translates おまけシナリオ as 'afterparty' while new correctly renders it as 'bonus scenario' — a meta-narrative term referring to extra story content, not a party.

TextENGNew: "Please, calm down, Maebara-san! I'll dispatch a patrol car to your location immediately! Just hold on for three minutes — you can do it!!"
"Three minutes?! I'm already surrounded... gyah, gyah!! Stop!! At least be gentle with me!!!"
*scuffle*!
"Would you hold still! A grown man who can't accept defeat gracefully!"
"Someone help!! Mooooom!!"
"Hau-au, Keiichi-kun is so cute even when he's struggling~~ I'm taking you home with me!!!"
"The moment you fled into this phone booth, you walked straight into my trap~!"
"Squeeeeze~~~~~!! Urghhhh..."
changeReason: Old translation adds '*thud*' which is not present in the Japanese original; new translation removes this addition, more faithfully representing the source.

TextENGNew: "Hello?! Hello?! Can you hear me, Maebara-san?!"
changeReason: Old translates the repeated もしもし/もしもーし as 'Are you there?' and 'Hello?' while new correctly renders both as 'Hello?' matching the Japanese phone greeting pattern.

TextENGNew: I knew all too well how distant that voice from the dangling receiver was.
But... I couldn't help shouting anyway.
And then, from the far end of the line... I heard a faint murmur.
"...Nipah~☆"
I don't know what he was saying.
...From the tone of voice... was it talking to himself?
Or was he addressing someone there with him?
I sharpened my senses... straining to catch something more...
What had he been trying to convey...?
Just what... on earth...!!!
*click* *beep* *beep* *beep* *beep*...
Cut off so abruptly. ...Had the ten-yen coin run out?!
It was a pay phone, after all...!!
And because it cut off so abruptly.
...The very last words were etched all the more vividly in my mind.
"'...Nipah~☆,' he said."
In that moment, something told me.
......That there was no longer any reason to hurry.
And so it was that one hapless young man was swallowed once more by the darkness of Hinamizawa...
